# WebWaka OS — Verticals Execution Prompts: Commerce P2 Batch 2

**Document type:** Agent execution prompt set
**Scope:** Commerce P2 verticals (Batch 2) — 12 verticals covered
**Repo:** https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os
**Branch base:** `main`
**Milestone targets:** M9–M10
**Status:** Ready for agent execution — all P1 verticals complete (M8b–M8e)
**Generated by:** MASTER_CONTINUATION_PROMPT.md — Set B

---

> **3-in-1 Platform Note:**
> Every vertical in this document serves at least **Pillar 1 (Ops)** and **Pillar 3 (Marketplace/Discovery)**.
> Verticals with Pillar 2 also activate `apps/brand-runtime/` (branded storefront or portfolio site).
> **SuperAgent AI is cross-cutting — it is NOT a fourth pillar.** All AI features route through `packages/superagent`.
> See `docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md` for the full pillar map.

### General rules for all agents using these prompts

- Never make assumptions about WebWaka's architecture, API contracts, or business logic. Always read the referenced documents and code first.
- Research deeply before executing. When encountering domain-specific patterns, do online research first and synthesise with Nigeria market context.
- Thoroughness is far more important than speed.
- All work must be pushed to GitHub. No local partial work should remain outside the repo.
- SuperAgent is the AI layer — all AI features in verticals route through `packages/superagent`. Never call AI providers directly from vertical code.
- Platform Invariants are non-negotiable. Read `docs/governance/platform-invariants.md` in full before implementing any vertical.
- The verticals FSM engine in `packages/verticals/` governs lifecycle states. All verticals must use it — no custom state machines.
- Every monetary value stored in the database must be an integer in kobo (P9). No decimals, no floats.
- No cross-tenant data leakage (T3). Every D1 query scopes to `tenant_id`.

---

## TASK V-COMM-EXT-B1: Construction Firm / Contractor Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `construction`
- **Priority:** P2-Top100 — Nigeria's construction sector contributes ~3% of GDP; thousands of registered and unregistered contractors; federal and state government projects are primary revenue driver
- **Milestone target:** M9
- **Primary pillars:** Pillar 1 (Ops) + Pillar 2 (Branding) + Pillar 3 (Marketplace)
- **`primary_pillars` value:** `["ops","branding","marketplace"]`
- **GitHub context:**
  - Verticals master plan: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/verticals-master-plan.md
  - 3-in-1 platform architecture: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md
  - Verticals FSM engine: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/packages/verticals/src/
  - Platform invariants: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/platform-invariants.md
  - Entitlement model: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/entitlement-model.md
  - AI integration framework: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/ai-integration-framework.md
  - SuperAgent product spec: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/superagent/02-product-spec.md

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in construction project management software and multi-tenant SaaS platforms, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:**
- Nigerian construction sector operations — contractor registration with BPP (Bureau of Public Procurement), COREN (Council for the Regulation of Engineering in Nigeria), CORBON (Contractors' Registration Board of Nigeria)
- Bill of Quantities (BoQ) in Nigerian construction context, government procurement rules, milestone-based payment tracking
- Project management for building, road, and infrastructure contractors in Nigeria
- Cloudflare Workers + Hono + D1 TypeScript strict mode
- WebWaka SuperAgent integration
- Verticals FSM lifecycle engine

---

**1. Mandatory context reading (100% before execution):**

- `docs/governance/verticals-master-plan.md` — entry for `construction`
- `docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md`
- `docs/governance/platform-invariants.md` — P2, P9, P10, P12, P13, T3, T4
- `infra/db/seeds/0004_verticals-master.csv` — row for `construction`
- `packages/verticals/src/` — FSM engine
- `packages/auth/src/guards.ts`
- `docs/governance/ai-integration-framework.md`
- `infra/db/migrations/` — next unused migration number

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks:
- Nigerian construction market — NBS data on construction sector GDP contribution, number of registered contractors with BPP and COREN, government vs private project split
- Top competitors: Buildersmart NG, ProCore (used by multinationals), local spreadsheet/WhatsApp management
- Key features: project register (name, client, location, contract value in kobo), milestone payment schedule, Bill of Quantities (BoQ) line items, contractor staff register, material procurement tracker, BPP registration certificate tracking
- COREN and CORBON registration, BPP contractor classification (Category A/B/C by contract value), state public works compliance

Execution plan:
- **Objective:** Give Nigerian construction contractors a digital project register, milestone payment tracker, BoQ management, and regulatory compliance records — all in kobo
- **Schema:**
  - `construction_profiles` — company_name, COREN_number, CORBON_number, BPP_registration, BPP_category (A/B/C), CAC_number, workspace_id, tenant_id, status
  - `construction_projects` — project_name, client_name, client_phone, location, contract_value_kobo, start_date, expected_end_date, status (bid/awarded/in_progress/completed/defects_liability), tenant_id
  - `construction_milestones` — project_id, milestone_name, amount_kobo, due_date, paid_date, status (pending/invoiced/paid), tenant_id
  - `construction_materials` — project_id, material_name, quantity, unit_cost_kobo, supplier, procurement_date, tenant_id
- **API routes:** register, profile GET, transition, projects CRUD, milestones CRUD, materials CRUD, ai-advisory
- **FSM states:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `coren_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `PROJECT_COST_ESTIMATION` (AI assists with material cost benchmarking) — L2 autonomy; Pro plan minimum given project values involved
- **Regulatory hook:** COREN number + CORBON number at `coren_verified`; BPP category stored
- **KYC:** Tier 3 (CAC + BVN + tax) for contracts above ₦10M; Tier 2 for standard projects

---

**3. Implementation workflow:**

Branch: `feat/vertical-construction` from `main`.

**3a. Schema:** `infra/db/migrations/[NEXT_NUMBER]_vertical_construction.sql` — `construction_profiles`, `construction_projects`, `construction_milestones`, `construction_materials`. All monetary fields in kobo (P9). `tenant_id NOT NULL` (T3).

**3b. Package:** `packages/verticals-construction/src/index.ts` — `registerConstructionVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`. FSM: `seeded` → `claimed` → `coren_verified` → `active` → `suspended`. AI: aggregate project counts + revenue by category — no client names, locations (P13). L2 autonomy cap.

**3c. Routes:** `apps/api/src/routes/verticals/construction.ts`. AI advisory blocked on USSD (P12). Mount in `apps/api/src/index.ts`.

**3d. T3:** All D1 queries scoped.

---

**4. QA and verification:**

**Test file:** `packages/verticals-construction/src/construction.test.ts`

Positive: register; FSM `seeded` → `coren_verified` with valid COREN+CORBON numbers; project created with integer contract_value_kobo (P9); milestone with integer amount_kobo (P9); material with integer unit_cost_kobo; milestone status: `pending` → `invoiced` → `paid`; AI cost benchmark with aiRights + NDPR. Negative: fractional kobo (P9); cross-tenant → 403 (T3); AI triply blocked (aiRights, NDPR, USSD); invalid FSM → typed error (T4); `coren_verified` without COREN → typed error. Security: unauth → 401; KYC → 403.

**Minimum test count: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Finalize and push:**

```bash
git commit -m "feat(vertical/construction): Construction vertical — project register, milestones, BoQ, AI cost estimation (M9)"
git push origin feat/vertical-construction
```
PR checklist: FSM, migration, `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`, labeled `3in1:pillar-1/2/3`, guards, SuperAgent, T3, ≥15 tests, zero typecheck/lint errors.

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in petroleum retail and fuel station management, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:**
- Nigerian downstream petroleum sector — NUPRC (formerly DPR) retail outlet licensing, pump calibration certification, PPPRA fuel pricing (deregulated from 2023)
- Daily litre-based sales tracking, stock reconciliation, pump attendant shift management
- All petroleum volumes tracked in litres; all monetary values in kobo (P9)
- Cloudflare Workers + Hono + D1 TypeScript strict mode
- WebWaka SuperAgent integration
- Verticals FSM lifecycle engine

---

**1. Mandatory context reading (100% before execution):**

- `docs/governance/verticals-master-plan.md` — entry for `fuel-station`
- `docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md`
- `docs/governance/platform-invariants.md` — P2, P9, P10, P12, P13, T3, T4
- `infra/db/seeds/0004_verticals-master.csv` — row for `fuel-station`
- `packages/verticals/src/` — FSM engine
- `packages/auth/src/guards.ts`
- `docs/governance/ai-integration-framework.md`
- `infra/db/migrations/` — next migration number

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks:
- Nigerian fuel station market — NUPRC figures on licensed retail outlets, Abuja/Lagos pricing post-subsidy removal, depot-to-station supply chain
- Competitors: no major dedicated Nigerian fuel station SaaS; management via tally sheets, some NNPC-affiliated apps
- Key features: daily pump reading log (opening meter, closing meter, litres sold per pump per product: PMS/AGO/DPK), stock tank level tracking, price-per-litre management (kobo per litre × 1000), attendant shift reconciliation, NUPRC licence tracking, daily revenue report
- NUPRC retail outlet licence, pump calibration cert (PPPRA/SON), corporate NNPC dealer agreement

Execution plan:
- **Objective:** Give every Nigerian filling station digital pump reading logs, stock tracking, attendant shift reconciliation, and NUPRC compliance records
- **Schema:**
  - `fuel_station_profiles` — station_name, NUPRC_licence, dealer_type (independent/nnpc_mega/total/ardova), CAC_number, state, LGA, workspace_id, tenant_id, status
  - `fuel_pumps` — station_id, pump_number, product (PMS/AGO/DPK), current_price_kobo_per_litre, tenant_id
  - `fuel_daily_readings` — pump_id, shift_date (unix), opening_meter, closing_meter, litres_sold, cash_received_kobo, attendant_name, tenant_id
  - `fuel_tank_stock` — station_id, product, capacity_litres, current_level_litres, last_delivery_litres, last_delivery_date, tenant_id
- **FSM states:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `nuprc_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `SALES_FORECAST` (daily litres demand trend), `DEMAND_PLANNING` (stock delivery timing) — L2; Growth plan
- **Regulatory hook:** NUPRC licence number + expiry at `nuprc_verified`
- **KYC:** Tier 3 for stations with retail credit accounts (fleet clients); Tier 2 for standard cash operations

---

**3. Implementation workflow:**

Branch: `feat/vertical-fuel-station` from `main`.

**3a. Schema:** `[NEXT_NUMBER]_vertical_fuel_station.sql`. Monetary fields in kobo (P9). Volumes in integer litres × 1000 (millilitres as integer) to avoid floats. `tenant_id NOT NULL` (T3).

**3b. Package:** `packages/verticals-fuel-station/src/index.ts` — `registerFuelStationVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`. AI: aggregate litres-sold and revenue totals per product — no attendant names or individual readings (P13).

**3c. Routes:** `apps/api/src/routes/verticals/fuel-station.ts`. AI blocked on USSD (P12).

**3d. T3:** All queries scoped.

---

**4. QA and verification:**

**Test file:** `packages/verticals-fuel-station/src/fuel-station.test.ts`

Positive: register; FSM to `nuprc_verified` with valid licence; pump reading created with integer values; litres in integer millilitres (no float); daily revenue in kobo; AI demand forecast with aiRights + NDPR. Negative: float volume rejected; fractional kobo rejected (P9); cross-tenant → 403 (T3); AI triply blocked; invalid FSM → typed error; `nuprc_verified` without licence → typed error. Security: unauth → 401; KYC → 403.

**Minimum test count: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Finalize and push:**

```bash
git commit -m "feat(vertical/fuel-station): Fuel Station vertical — pump readings, NUPRC compliance, AI demand forecast (M9)"
git push origin feat/vertical-fuel-station
```
PR checklist: FSM, migration, `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`, labeled `3in1:pillar-1/3`, guards, SuperAgent, T3, ≥15 tests, zero errors.

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks:
- Nigerian property development market — NBS housing deficit (~28 million units), off-plan vs completed unit sales ratio, average estate unit price in Lekki vs Abuja vs Port Harcourt in naira (× 100 for kobo)
- Competitor tools: Estateintel, BuyLetLive (listing), Diaspora real estate platforms; development management mostly in Excel
- Key features: estate/project register, unit types (1bed/2bed/3bed/duplex), unit allocation to buyers, instalment payment schedule in kobo, governor's consent tracking, C of O tracking, construction milestone photos reference, buyer KYC status
- SURCON registration, TOPREC approval, CAC (RC number), LASG/FCT estate permit

Execution plan:
- **Objective:** Give Nigerian property developers a digital estate register, unit allocation system, buyer instalment payment tracker, and regulatory document management — all monetary in kobo
- **Schema:**
  - `property_developer_profiles` — company_name, SURCON_number, TOPREC_number, CAC_RC, workspace_id, tenant_id, status
  - `property_estates` — estate_name, location, state, lga, land_title_type (C_of_O/Deed/Gazette/Excision), permit_number, total_units, workspace_id, tenant_id, status
  - `property_units` — estate_id, unit_type, unit_number, floor_area_sqm, price_kobo, status (available/reserved/allocated/completed), tenant_id
  - `property_allocations` — unit_id, buyer_phone, buyer_name, total_price_kobo, deposit_kobo, instalment_plan (JSON: [{amount_kobo, due_date}]), status (active/completed/defaulted), tenant_id
- **FSM states:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `surcon_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `PROPERTY_VALUATION_ASSIST` (market price benchmarking) — L2; Pro plan (given high-value transactions)
- **Regulatory hook:** SURCON + TOPREC numbers at `surcon_verified`; CAC RC also required
- **KYC:** Tier 3 mandatory — property transactions involve high-value transfers; full CAC + BVN + tax compliance required

---

**3. Implementation workflow:**

**3a. Schema:** `[NEXT_NUMBER]_vertical_property_developer.sql`. All prices in kobo (P9). Instalment amounts in kobo. `tenant_id NOT NULL` on all tables (T3).

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in real estate agency and property management software, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:**
- Nigerian real estate brokerage — Lagos Island/Mainland rental markets, Abuja FCT lettings, agent commission structures (typically 10% for sale, 5–10% for rental), NIESV membership, ESVARBON registration
- Property listing management, landlord-tenant pipeline, commission tracking in kobo
- Cloudflare Workers + Hono + D1 TypeScript strict mode
- WebWaka SuperAgent integration
- Verticals FSM lifecycle engine

---

**1. Mandatory context reading (100% before execution):**

- `docs/governance/verticals-master-plan.md` — entry for `real-estate-agency`
- `docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md`
- `docs/governance/platform-invariants.md` — P2, P9, P10, P12, P13, T3, T4
- `infra/db/seeds/0004_verticals-master.csv` — row for `real-estate-agency`
- `packages/verticals/src/` — FSM engine
- `packages/auth/src/guards.ts`
- `docs/governance/ai-integration-framework.md`
- `infra/db/migrations/` — next migration number

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks:
- Nigerian real estate agency market — NIESV membership figures, property listings on PropertyPro, Jiji.ng, NigeriaPropertyCentre; Lagos vs Abuja rental price trajectories
- Competitors: PropertyPro (listing-focused), Nigeria Property Centre, private Jiji agents; no dedicated CRM for Nigerian agents
- Key features: property listing (type, location, price_per_annum_kobo or sale_price_kobo, bedrooms, amenities), client pipeline (buyer/tenant enquiries), viewings scheduler, offer/negotiation tracking, commission calculation, tenancy agreement reference, landlord relationship management

Execution plan:
- **Objective:** Give Nigerian real estate agents a digital property portfolio, client pipeline, viewing scheduler, and commission tracker — with Pillar 3 public listing discovery
- **Schema:**
  - `real_estate_agency_profiles` — agency_name, NIESV_number, ESVARBON_number, CAC_number, workspace_id, tenant_id, status
  - `property_listings` — title, type (flat/duplex/bungalow/land/commercial), transaction_type (sale/rent), state, lga, address, price_kobo, bedrooms, bathrooms, status (available/under_offer/let/sold), tenant_id
  - `property_enquiries` — listing_id, client_phone, client_name, enquiry_type (viewing/offer/rent), offer_price_kobo, status (new/viewing_scheduled/offer_made/accepted/closed), tenant_id
  - `property_commissions` — listing_id, transaction_type, gross_value_kobo, commission_rate_pct, commission_kobo, status (pending/invoiced/received), tenant_id
- **FSM states:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `niesv_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `PROPERTY_VALUATION_ASSIST` (comparable listing price benchmarking), `SALES_FORECAST` (pipeline conversion rate) — L2; Growth plan
- **Regulatory hook:** NIESV number + ESVARBON number at `niesv_verified`
- **KYC:** Tier 2 for commission invoicing; Tier 3 for property escrow handling

---

**3. Implementation workflow:**

Branch: `feat/vertical-real-estate-agency` from `main`.

**3a. Schema:** `[NEXT_NUMBER]_vertical_real_estate_agency.sql`. All monetary in kobo (P9). `tenant_id NOT NULL` (T3). Index on `status`, `transaction_type`.

**3b. Package:** `packages/verticals-real-estate-agency/src/index.ts` — `registerRealEstateAgencyVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`. AI: aggregate listing counts and price ranges — no client PII (P13).

**3c. Routes:** Full CRUD for profiles, listings, enquiries, commissions. AI blocked on USSD (P12).

**3d. T3:** All queries scoped.

---

**4. QA and verification:**

**Test file:** `packages/verticals-real-estate-agency/src/real-estate-agency.test.ts`

Positive: register; FSM to `niesv_verified`; listing with integer price_kobo; enquiry with offer_price_kobo; commission calculation correct in kobo; AI price benchmark with aiRights + NDPR. Negative: fractional kobo; cross-tenant → 403; AI triply blocked; invalid FSM → typed error. Security: unauth → 401; KYC → 403.

**Minimum test count: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Finalize and push:**

```bash
git commit -m "feat(vertical/real-estate-agency): Real Estate Agency vertical — listings, pipeline, commission tracker (M9)"
git push origin feat/vertical-real-estate-agency
```
PR checklist: FSM, migration, `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`, labeled, guards, SuperAgent, T3, ≥15 tests, zero errors.

  - `spa_services` — service_name, category (massage/facial/body/nail), duration_minutes, price_kobo, tenant_id
  - `spa_appointments` — client_phone, service_id, therapist_id, room_number, appointment_time, status (booked/confirmed/in_session/completed/cancelled/no_show), deposit_kobo, tenant_id
  - `spa_memberships` — client_phone, package_name, monthly_fee_kobo, sessions_per_month, sessions_used, valid_until, tenant_id
- **FSM states:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `permit_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `APPOINTMENT_OPTIMIZATION` (peak hour analysis), `CUSTOMER_SEGMENTATION` (loyalty vs lapsed) — L2; Growth plan
- **Regulatory hook:** NASC number + state health permit at `permit_verified`
- **KYC:** Tier 1 for appointment deposits; Tier 2 for membership subscriptions above ₦200,000/year

---

**3. Implementation workflow:**

Branch: `feat/vertical-spa` from `main`.

**3a. Schema:** `[NEXT_NUMBER]_vertical_spa.sql`. All monetary in kobo (P9). `tenant_id NOT NULL` (T3).

**3b. Package:** `packages/verticals-spa/src/index.ts` — `registerSpaVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`. AI: aggregate booking counts and session revenue — no client phone or health intake data (P13 strictly enforced for wellness PII).

**3c. Routes:** Full CRUD. AI blocked on USSD (P12).

**3d. T3:** All queries scoped.

---

**4. QA and verification:**

**Test file:** `packages/verticals-spa/src/spa.test.ts`

Positive: register; FSM to `permit_verified`; appointment with integer deposit_kobo; membership with integer monthly_fee_kobo; appointment status progression; AI utilisation analysis with aiRights + NDPR. Negative: fractional kobo; cross-tenant → 403; AI triply blocked; invalid FSM → typed error. Security: unauth → 401; KYC → 403.

**Minimum test count: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Finalize and push:**

```bash
git commit -m "feat(vertical/spa): Spa vertical — appointments, memberships, therapist scheduling, AI utilisation (M10)"
git push origin feat/vertical-spa
```
PR checklist: FSM, migration, `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`, labeled, guards, SuperAgent, T3, ≥15 tests, zero errors.

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in fashion and bespoke tailoring management software, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:**
- Nigerian tailoring and fashion design sector — Aso-Ebi group orders (10–200 dresses from one fabric), Lagos Island high-fashion designers (Ituen Basi, Maki Oh), Aba ready-to-wear cluster, Kano Babban Riga tailors
- Customer measurement tracking, sewing order pipeline, fabric procurement, deposit and balance payment
- Cloudflare Workers + Hono + D1 TypeScript strict mode
- WebWaka SuperAgent integration
- Verticals FSM lifecycle engine

---

**1. Mandatory context reading (100% before execution):**

- `docs/governance/verticals-master-plan.md` — entry for `tailor`
- `docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md`
- `docs/governance/platform-invariants.md` — P2, P9, P10, P12, P13, T3, T4

- `packages/auth/src/guards.ts`
- `docs/governance/ai-integration-framework.md`
- `infra/db/migrations/` — next migration number

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks:
- Nigerian tailoring market — estimated number of tailors/fashion designers (SMEDAN data), aso-ebi annual volume, Lagos Fashion Week participants, average bespoke outfit price range
- Competitors: no dedicated tailoring SaaS in Nigeria; WhatsApp order management is universal; FashionGo (US), Tailorbird (global but not Nigeria-focused)
- Key features: client measurement records (bust/waist/hip/etc — stored as client profile), order intake (style description, fabric type, delivery_date, total_price_kobo, deposit_kobo), fabric stock management (metres, price per metre in kobo), Aso-Ebi group order management, production status tracking

Execution plan:
- **Objective:** Give Nigerian tailors digital order management, client measurement records, fabric stock tracking, Aso-Ebi group order coordination, and AI demand forecasting for peak seasons
- **Schema:**
  - `tailor_profiles` — business_name, type (bespoke/aso-ebi/ready-to-wear/all), CAC_or_trade_assoc_number, state, lga, workspace_id, tenant_id, status
  - `tailor_clients` — client_phone, measurements (JSON: {bust, waist, hip, shoulder, sleeve, thigh, inseam} in cm × 10 integer), tenant_id
  - `tailor_orders` — client_id, style_description, fabric_type, delivery_date, price_kobo, deposit_kobo, balance_kobo, status (intake/cutting/sewing/finishing/ready/collected), tenant_id
  - `tailor_fabric_stock` — fabric_name, colour, fabric_type, metres_available (integer cm), cost_per_metre_kobo, supplier, tenant_id
- **FSM states:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `active` (3-state for informal operators; advanced: `seeded` → `claimed` → `cac_verified` → `active` — choose based on seed CSV)
- **SuperAgent:** `DEMAND_PLANNING` (seasonal Aso-Ebi peak forecasting), `CUSTOMER_SEGMENTATION` — L2; Growth plan
- **Regulatory hook:** CAC or trade association number (optional for informal operators); state trade union membership
- **KYC:** Tier 1 for informal tailors; Tier 2 for bulk Aso-Ebi contracts above ₦500,000

---

**3. Implementation workflow:**

Branch: `feat/vertical-tailor` from `main`.

**3a. Schema:** `[NEXT_NUMBER]_vertical_tailor.sql`. All monetary in kobo (P9). Measurements as integers (cm × 10) to avoid floats. `tenant_id NOT NULL` (T3).

**3b. Package:** `packages/verticals-tailor/src/index.ts` — `registerTailorVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`. AI: aggregate order counts and revenue by style category — no client measurements or phone numbers (P13).

**3c. Routes:** Full CRUD. AI blocked on USSD (P12).

**3d. T3:** All queries scoped.

---

**4. QA and verification:**

**Test file:** `packages/verticals-tailor/src/tailor.test.ts`

Positive: register; FSM transitions; order with integer price_kobo, deposit_kobo, balance_kobo (P9); measurements as integers; order status progression; Aso-Ebi group order; AI seasonal forecast with aiRights + NDPR. Negative: fractional kobo; float measurements rejected; cross-tenant → 403; AI triply blocked; invalid FSM → typed error. Security: unauth → 401; KYC → 403.

**Minimum test count: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Finalize and push:**

```bash
git commit -m "feat(vertical/tailor): Tailor vertical — order pipeline, measurements, Aso-Ebi groups, AI demand (M10)"
git push origin feat/vertical-tailor
```
